g.text: A neuron is a tiny calculator. It takes several numbers as

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: input, multiplies each input by a 'weight' (its importance),

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: adds a bias value (adjustment / offset), and finally, passes

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: this result through an activation function to produce one

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: A single neuron is simple, but a layer combines many neurons,

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: each with its own weights. They all get the same input but

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: produce different outputs—letting the layer detect many

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: In this layer, a neuron has 3

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: inputs, one for each neuron in

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 1. Run sample through network with the forward pass

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: - Compare the networks prediction with the real label, and calculate a loss.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: (Number representing how good/bad the network is at predicting)

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 2. An optimizer determines how weight and bias values should be changed, to improve the loss

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: - Calculate partial derivatives for all weights and biases (back propagation)

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: - Learning rate is how big of a change is made to weights and biases at a time (overshoot/speed tradeoff)

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 3. Run inference again, and monitor loss over time to see if network improves

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 4. When network is trained, it should know samples well, and be able to predict sample labels not seen before

g.text: Gradient descent is the core optimization algorithm used to train neural networks. Its

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: primary goal is to minimize a cost function (or loss function), which measures the difference

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: between the network's predictions and the actual target values.
